programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Find out how the Pandemic has affected Boston's housing market

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Do you have a million dollars? It may cost you that much to buy a condo in Boston. Meanwhile, city dwellers looking for space in the suburbs are also facing the highest home prices on record. And if you’re looking to rent, it’s rough out there! Apartment vacancies of 2020 are no longer available in 2021. Tonight, a look at the housing headaches in Greater Boston, including stories of those laid off during the pandemic and the organizations helping them stay in their homes.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle’s award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We eat it, we play on it, and on lakes and ponds all around New England, people fish on it. Tonight, we’re talking all things…ice! We tour the elaborate Ice Castles in the woods of North Woodstock, N.H., and meet an aspiring Olympian during a visit to the new “home ice” of the Skating Club of Boston. We get a lesson in old-school ice harvesting, and StormTeam 5 meteorologist A.J. Burnett gives a lesson on the science of ice. And if all of that has worked up an appetite? We try “shaved snow” from Snowdaes in Lowell.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Caught up in “Top Gun: Maverick” fever? Now’s your chance to see if life aboard a U.S. Navy aircraft carrier is anything like the movies. Our camera follows Anthony Everett for a quick hitch aboard the USS Truman - from the mess hall, to the flight deck, and everywhere in between. Channel your inner Maverick (Iceman?) during this look back at a once-in-a-lifetime adventure.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: These days Spain is breaking tourism records. Tonight we’ll take a tour of Spain’s visually stunning and culturally rich cities, and hear from locals about the positives (and a few negatives) of all that international attention

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we talk to filmmakers and organizers about the oldest independent film festival in New England - and screen clips of films created by New England filmmakers for whom an indie festival may be their best shot at the big-time.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Tonight Anthony Everett is pedaling away on an “inn-to-inn” tour of Vermont’s Champlain Valley. From Brandon to Vergennes he takes in mountain vistas, checks out the “most beautiful street” and the “best ice cream” (no shortage of local pride here!) and shares the stories of innkeepers and other Vermonters he meets along the way. Hard-core cyclist or more comfortable on an e-bike? Either way, locals say – you can enjoy point-to-point hospitality in the Green Mountain State.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ever wonder what kind of genius is being conjured inside the labs at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology? Tonight we take a peek and discover everything from video games to futuristic fashion to… paper airplanes!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Massachusetts has a long history of land conservation. Now local nonprofits, volunteers, and advocates are leading the charge to “re-wild” forests – returning them to their natural condition while protecting biodiverse habitats. We visit Mass Audubon’s Tidmarsh Wildlife Sanctuary in Plymouth, and see how the Native Land Conservancy is restoring parts of the Muddy Pond Preserve in Kingston. At Walden Pond, high schoolers work to understand Henry David Thoreau’s message of self-reliance and sustainability. We also explore how a local land trust is using conservation land to help feed families who are food-insecure.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Shift: The Ragbrai Documentary
programme.desc: A celebration of the oldest, largest, and longest recreational touring bicycle ride in the world. Three riders and a pair of community leaders reach new personal heights, changing their lives along the seven day ride across Iowa.

programme.title: WCVB 50 Years Special
programme.desc: (2022) WCVB Channel 5 celebrates 50 years - from the very first moments on the air to meeting the people who make it all happen.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: “Paintings” that are actually made of throwaway plastic. A bakery where they’re jumping in on the “Is It Cake?” trend. And a museum based on illusions. Tonight, a journey of the mind, which can trick us into thinking one thing is actually another.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Cold temps and cabin fever got you down? Stick an umbrella in your drink and join us for a return trip to some of Chronicle’s island destinations. Anthony explores some of the 365 beaches on the island of Antigua (one for every day of the year). Shayna Seymour climbs aboard a catamaran to sail the waters around Barbados. And Ted Reinstein finds a little R&R right nearby in Bermuda. New England beach weather may be six months off but this island tour may help tide you over!

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: Who knew you could explore endless outdoor adventure, a vibrant food and wine scene, crystal blue freshwater, world-class golf, and a charming downtown- all in Northern Michigan? Join host Hannah Cumler as she showcases the best of Traverse City!

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Wormtown, Shovel Town, Whip City - Massachusetts folks love a nickname, and we’re back for Round 2 of testing your nickname knowledge. It’s a journey through town monikers that are less obvious than the Witch City (Salem).

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Stories of everyday New Englanders doing extraordinary things in their own “Labors of Love.” Ted Reinstein revisits Boston’s old West End with a man pushing to memorialize one of the neighborhood’s native sons -- the legendary actor, Leonard Nimoy. Ted hops a sailboat with Sail Cape Cod which is helping those with challenges find a bit of respite on the open waters. He treks to the Cape Ann Museum where they’re honoring Gloucester fishing families through art. Legos to preserve history? It’s happening in Rhode Island. Finally, Ted also finds free bikes in Lowell for those who need them.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is back with more stories of people pursuing their passions. Ted meets a man who is devoted to keeping the memory of fallen Massachusetts veterans alive. He later reconnects with the couple working to restore the cabin of a pioneering author and early civil rights figure.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is back with all new stories of New Englanders following their passions.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You’ve heard of Oprah’s Book Club but have you heard of the Bad *B* Book Club? Started by a local, self-proclaimed bookworm, the club has one requirement: selections must have been written by women. In honor of Women’s History Month, a celebration of local female authors, readers, and lovers of the written word. We’ll also meet a local writer, originally from Malawi, who said writing her book allowed her to discover her true self.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: She was known as the “Lady of the Dunes,” and her identity remained a mystery for 48 years, until just this past Halloween. Chronicle has been following the case for decades and tonight Anthony Everett revisits the investigation into the remains of a woman found in the remote dunes of Provincetown. Anthony looks into just who victim Ruth Marie Terry was, speaks with law enforcement officials who never gave up the search for her identity, and examines the investigation that now includes the bizarre history of Terry’s husband. A look into where this investigation stands after nearly five decades of work.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Lake Sunapee is sometimes overshadowed by the bigger lakes in New Hampshire, but it has long been a draw for summer vacationers seeking peace and quiet, outdoor activity, and scenic New England charm. Tonight Ted Reinstein is cruising the Sunapee Scenic Byway, a 39-mile route that winds through the towns of Sunapee, Newbury, and New London. In Sunapee Harbor he steps back in time with a cruise aboard the steamboat M.V. Kearsarge and in Newbury he explores the historic John Hay Estate. And there’s no snow on the ground, but Mount Sunapee is still buzzing with activity.

programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, two men wheeled what looked like an IBM computer into a Lake Tahoe casino. The events that followed rocked the industry, baffled bomb technicians, and changed the lives of a criminal’s and a casino magnate’s family forever.
